item.title: Re: Attribution, Thanks and Other Matters
item.content:encoded: I know it's been like, years since I said this but if anyone doesn't know Aoneareyou60 is me. I honestly wanna say thank you so much for turning one of my ideas into a SCP.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded: Noted. Will include with other crosslink edits.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
I accept. In addition, I will include this following crosslink with Document-2481 here:

A tribe which allegedly existed during the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors period in China. Its most famous member is the warlord Chiyou, revered by ethnic Miao as a mythical king.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The edit proposed is to link the article to the Coalition hub page, as the article makes significant reference to the GOI.

Editing this:

The following document was recovered from Global Occult Coalition PHYSICS Division Threat Entity Database by undercover agents.

To this:

The following document was recovered from Global Occult Coalition PHYSICS Division Threat Entity Database by undercover agents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for catching that. I think I mixed up the term back then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three Emperors and Five Sovereigns period in China

Was that intentional? Wikipedia lists a Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors period, not the other way around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the GOC document, and I think this would benefit from more tests (Why no cat bus?), but other than that, I really like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the odd article where I don't find the backstory quite as interesting as the effect itself. But boy, what an original concept. Love that final test, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor nitpick, GOC Assessment teams use 3-digit unit identifiers beginning with a seven. (And now I feel like a nerd.)

For the record, I typed in a number beginning with "7" without knowing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Better than the first time I saw it!

Minor nitpick, GOC Assessment teams use 3-digit unit identifiers beginning with a seven. (And now I feel like a nerd.)

+1, would take out for a spin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nicely done MrWrong. I like that you added the moaning sounds/funeral music inside the cars, that was a nice touch.

+1

and determined that no bodies have been interned in the area.

should be "interred"

item.title: Attribution, Thanks and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
This was inspired Aoneareyou60 of reddit, who issued a prompt on "Roadkill Revenge" here ://www.reddit.com/r/SCP/comments/4kpv98/another_scp_idea_roadkill_revenge/

I did modify the initial prompt heavily since then…

Thank you to reviewers such as Hercules Rockefeller, Scorpion451, Steeltitan, Moen and others.

This article features the following:

  • SCP-2372 (Soulmates) by MrWrong
+ show block
– hide block

So this SCP somehow ended up in my re-using "Old Chin", a character mentioned in SCP-2372. I think it is befitting, as both SCPs deal with Chinese supernatural elements to some extent.

The LTE-1985-Grey Bluechild are reminiscent of Chiyou's followers as described in Document-2481, who essentially sided with the Brass Dragon and gained bodies of metal.

The road here is a residue of different thaumaturgies mixed onto one place, the Taoist-style reanimation and Brass Dragon's blessings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The transformation that happens to vehicles reminds me very strongly of Living Hell by Catherine Jinks1 (which is probably unpopular enough that no one even knows what it is) but it's in a very good way.

Footnotes
1. "But Cyan that book is for ages 12 and up" shhh I read it when I was young man
